events.1535: Bahadur Shah captured the fort of Chittor and then marched against the Mughal emperor.
events.1673: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj conquered the fort of Panhala.
events.1833: Vishwanath Narayan Mandalik, famous journalist, writer and social reformer, was born.
events.1864: Hari Narayan Apte, a well known Marathi writer who popularised social and historical themes in his novels, was born. He presided over the 8th Marathi Sahitya Sammelen in 1912.
events.1921: Sahir Ludhiyanvi, famous film lyricist, was born.
events.1930: Mahatma Gandhi started civil disobedience movement continues in India.
events.1941: Sir Jorge Abraham Gearson, philologist and archealogist, passed away.
events.1948: Air India International was established for overseas services.
events.1957: Balasaheb Gangadhar Kher, prominent leader in Maharashtra and former Chief Minister of Mumbai province, passed away.
events.1963: Gursharan Singh Narainsingh, cricketer (Indian batsman played one Test 1989-90), was born in Amritsar.
events.1986: L.K. Advani elected President of BJP.
events.1995: Dr. Duranand Kanhan Pillai, great litterateur of Malyali language, died.
events.1997: G. K. Moopanar unanimously elected first president of Tamil Maanila Congress.
events.1998: Indian Airlines operates two international flights with all women crew.
events.1999: The President formally revokes the proclamation imposing Central rule in Bihar after this was decided by the Union Cabinet earlier in the day.
events.2000: The Gujarat Government withdraws its January 3 notification, thus reimposing the ban on its employees from participating in RSS activities.
